purpose: Truth matrix for note-quality-check's deterministic core: Nahbereich candidacy (0-byte / whitespace-only ONLY), the five intentional-content signals as survival guards, yaml-sanity exclusion routing, and protected files/folders. Wired into scripts/test-note-quality-check.sh.

dispositions.delete-candidate: Eligible for permanent delete (0 bytes) — ONLY after the preview/user gate. Never executed pre-preview.
dispositions.exclude-sanity: Broken YAML (shape-beta, duplicate keys, multi-block, unclosed) — excluded from scoring + finding. A corrupted file is a repair case, not a low-quality note.
dispositions.protected: Never processed or scored (_vault-autopilot.md, _-prefixed root files, _trash/ contents).
dispositions.review: Goes through the normal cluster walk-through. The skill may suggest Keep or Archive; only the user can say Trash.
dispositions.trash-candidate: Eligible for soft-delete to _trash/ (whitespace-only) — ONLY after the preview/user gate.

notes.fail-safe default: Only literally-empty content (0 bytes, pure whitespace) is ever a Nahbereich candidate. Every cell with ANY non-whitespace content — even one generic-named line with no signal (Untitled 5) — is 'review'. Deleting a valuable note is the worst failure class; uncertainty always resolves to KEEP/DEFER.
notes.survival cells: Untitled (one wikilink), New Note (one embed), Quick Note (frontmatter only), Draft (3 plain lines), 2024 Tax Strategy Notes (descriptive name, one line) all LOOK trashy to a naive substance heuristic and MUST survive — the signal column pins WHY each one lives.
notes.signal precedence: embed > wikilink > frontmatter > lines3 > filename > none (reporting order only; any single signal is sufficient to guard the note).
